briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Even in slick calm clear conditions, a jerkbait over the top of them will draw fish from distance. 'They're going to see it and they're going to come.' Don't switch to finesse just because it's calm.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Even in slick calm clear conditions, a jerkbait over the top of them will draw fish from distance. 'They're going to see it and they're going to come.' Don't switch to finesse just because it's c
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'Fall is an especially great jerkbait time — forage has been growing all year, so use bigger jerkbaits. Always fish windy areas over calm spots — wind makes bass aggressive and looking up. Start
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'When fishing expansive flats with breaks, mark waypoints at each catch. The pattern often isn't the obvious points — it's tapering breaks with inside turns cutting into the breakline. Those inside turns concentrate fish. Use a crankbait to s...
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

The D Shad's extra density allows use on casting gear (15-lb fluorocarbon, medium-heavy rod) unlike most soft jerkbaits — this opens up fishing thick cover without breakoffs, and enables skip casting under docks and overhangs
Robertson: In fall, smallmouth stack up on boulder fields in 20 feet of water where baitfish are concentrated. The fish can be found within a very small 60-foot area when they're grouped up.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish follow but won't commit, extend your pause to 8-10 seconds and vary the cadence
